summary:
Xanthogranulomatous cholecystitis is considered a pre-malignant condition due to its role as an advanced form of chronic inflammation with sustained fibrotic changes and active immune response. This prolonged inflammatory environment promotes carcinogenic processes similar to those seen in other known precancerous conditions.  

(Note: The summary focuses only on clinically significant facts related directly to malignancy progression.)
